sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: The sample states that 'Your Risk' is only for the heritable component of the disease, and that environmental, lifestyle, and other non-heritable factors also affect overall susceptibility risk.
sampleRows.description: The sample uses lung cancer as an example, saying lung cancer is only 10% heritable and most cases are associated with lifestyle factors such as smoking.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Sibling Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample report
sampleRows.plainEnglishMeaning: Extracted from the public Sequencing.com Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample PDF; used here as sibling output-structure evidence for Developmental Disorders, not as a direct Developmental Disorders sample report or local developmental and neurodevelopmental output structure, autism, ADHD, developmental-delay, intellectual-disability, learning, language, behavior, neurologic, psychiatric, school, therapy, medication, prognosis, severity, family-risk, testing, or actionability guidance.

sampleRows.geneticAnalysis: Visible sample rows include Anxiety Related Behaviors Elevated; ADHD Typical; Autism Spectrum Disorder Reduced; Bipolar Disorder Typical; Cluster Headaches Typical; Depression Elevated; Dyslexia Typical; Epilepsy Typical; Major Depressive Disorder Elevated; Narcolepsy Typical; OCD Reduced; Schizophrenia Elevated; Tourette's Syndrome Typical.
sampleRows.description: The page explains that CACNA1C and CACNB2 help regulate calcium flow in brain cells and are among genes considered for several neuro-psychological diseases.
sampleRows.sourceLabel: Sibling Disease Risk Genetic Test Report sample report
